title: Colymbada davisii
intro: Colymbada davisii, also known as Davis's Coltsfoot, is a perennial plant species in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) native to the mountainous regions of California, USA. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may aid in the relief of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. The bioactive constituents of Colymbada davisii include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In herbal medicine, Colymbada davisii is often prepared as a tincture, tea, or infusion, and is sometimes used to make a cough syrup or expectorant to help relieve respiratory congestion.
taxonomy: Colymbada davisii is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion tribe within the daisy and sunflower family (Asteraceae). Colymbada davisii is native to the Iberian Peninsula. It is a perennial plant that belongs to the genus Colymbada and the family Asteraceae. The classification of Colymbada davisii is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Family: Asteraceae, Genus: Colymbada, Species: C. davisii. Colymbada davisii is also known as Jacobaea davisii or Senecio davisii in some classifications.
